name: Frequency analysis of discrete-time signals
task: Create /tmp/eval_dsp/dsp.py with three functions for analyzing a real discrete-time signal x of length N sampled at fs Hz. magnitude_spectrum(x, fs) — return (freqs, mags) for the one-sided spectrum: freqs has length N//2+1 with freqs[k] = k*fs/N; mags[k] = |X[k]|/N for k=0 (and Nyquist if N is even), else 2*|X[k]|/N — so a pure sine of amplitude A appears at its frequency with magnitude ≈ A. peak_frequency(x, fs) — returns the freq with the largest magnitude. apply_window(x, window='hann') — return (x_windowed, w) where w is the window of length N; supported windows: 'rectangular', 'hann' (0.5 - 0.5 cos(2πn/(N-1))), 'hamming' (0.54 - 0.46 cos(2πn/(N-1))). Implement the DFT yourself using cmath. Pure Python.

validation.script: import importlib.util,math; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_dsp/dsp.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); MS, PF, AW = mod.magnitude_spectrum, mod.peak_frequency, mod.apply_window; fs = 1000; f0 = 50; A = 2.0; N = 200; x = [A*math.sin(2*math.pi*f0*n/fs) for n in range(N)]; freqs, mags = MS(x, fs); assert PF(x, fs) == 50.0; assert abs(max(mags) - A) < 0.1; f1, f2 = 100, 200; A1, A2 = 1.0, 0.5; x2 = [A1*math.sin(2*math.pi*f1*n/fs) + A2*math.sin(2*math.pi*f2*n/fs) for n in range(N)]; fs_arr, ms = MS(x2, fs); bin_f1 = ms[fs_arr.index(100)]; bin_f2 = ms[fs_arr.index(200)]; assert abs(bin_f1 - A1) < 0.1 and abs(bin_f2 - A2) < 0.1; xw, w = AW(x, 'hann'); assert len(xw) == N and abs(w[0]) < 1e-9 and abs(w[-1]) < 1e-9; xw2, w2 = AW(x, 'hamming'); assert abs(w2[0] - 0.08) < 1e-3; print('OK')
